title: Longitudinal EEG Test-Retest Reliability in Healthy Individuals

funding_references.funder_name: Comité para el Desarrollo de la Investigación - CODI, Universidad de Antioquia
Comité para el Desarrollo de la Investigación (CODI), Universidad de Antioquia

description: This longitudinal dataset comprises resting-state EEG recordings from 43 healthy adults collected across four sessions over approximately two years (mean interval 7.2 months). The dataset was designed to assess test-retest reliability of quantitative EEG measures using automated preprocessing including independent component analysis and wavelet-enhanced artifact removal, enabling investigation of neural component stability and regional power dynamics.
methods_description: Resting-state EEG recordings were collected from 43 healthy participants across four longitudinal sessions spanning approximately two years with an average interval of 7.2 months between sessions. Data were processed using an automated preprocessing pipeline incorporating independent component analysis and wavelet-enhanced artifact removal. EEG data are provided in BIDS-compliant format with raw signals and associated metadata describing recording parameters.
